title: Fermented Flaky Middle Eastern Turkey Neck Crockpot Crumble
description: This dish is perfect for a cozy date night featuring fermented flaky texture from smoked turkey necks with a tadka seasoning blend. Suitable for jain followers.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup finely chopped fresh mint leaves

instructions: Preheat your crockpot (or stovetop) to low heat.
In a small bowl, mix together cumin seeds, coriander seeds, minced garlic, turmeric powder, and salt. Add ghee and mix well until the spices are evenly coated with oil.

Place turkey necks in the crockpot (or on stovetop), pour the seasoned ghee mixture over them, then cover with a lid and cook for 6-8 hours or until the meat is falling off the bone. Stir occasionally during cooking.

Once cooked, remove the turkey necks from the crockpot or pot, place them in a plate, and let it cool slightly before scraping the inside of the crockpot (or container) to get all the flavorful paste left behind. Discard any bones or debris.
In a mixing bowl, combine cooled turkey neck meat with cilantro and mint leaves. Mix thoroughly until everything is well combined.
Garnish with additional fresh herbs like parsley if desired for a vibrant look.
